title: cerastium papuanum subsp. keysseri
intro: Cerastium papuanum subsp. keysseri, also known as Keysser's chickweed, is a rare, herbaceous plant species within the Cerastium genus, native to New Guinea. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Keysser's chickweed include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing, making it a potential treatment for various skin conditions. Cerastium papuanum subsp. keysseri can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, allowing for topical application and internal consumption to harness its medicinal properties.
